human_verification.notes: Verified incident response specialty boundary, authoritative source coverage, systems-of-record requirements, output fidelity, refusal rules, hold/release escalation, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

review_notes: All 17 scenarios pass with explicit incident response evidence, advisory recommendation, escalation, refusal, hold/release, and tenant-specific behavior.
All required abilities across shared, quality-safety-and-assurance, and cross-industry layers are fully covered.

specialist_owned_evidence: Incident response outputs are tied to named incident tickets, timelines, telemetry, affected assets, owners, severity matrices, evidence stores, response stages, and control records.
The lane refuses scanning, exploitation, forensic tool operation, live remediation, incident command, legal/regulatory final language, and audit attestation.
Hold, quarantine, recovery release, production release, and finding closure boundaries require authorized owner evidence.
Post-incident review produces root-cause and control-gap hypotheses, not unsupported final attribution or executive decisions.
Runtime remains specification-based; retained generated implementation requires separate frequent-use promotion evidence.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after constraining jurisdictional and sector reporting questions to legal/regulatory routing and tenant-specific source retrieval.
Pack is suitable for validated spec-pack deployment and on-demand materialization. It can be promoted to a retained implementation only through a separate explicit build decision.
